title: I prayed, at first, a little Girl, by Emily Dickinson — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
I prayed, at first, a little Girl,
Because they told me to --
But stopped, when qualified to guess
How prayer would feel -- to me --

If I believed God looked around,
Each time my Childish eye
Fixed full, and steady, on his own
meaning: "I prayed, at first, a little Girl," by Emily Dickinson is a deep poem consisting of 25 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "I prayed, at first, a little Girl, Because they told me to --...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Dickinson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
